db_connection: CODE-ONLY — db_connection_results was empty in the supplied extraction and no live DB CLI session was available in this analysis context. No psql/sqlcmd run was attempted because no extraction artifact recorded one. HOWEVER, Phase 4 spot check of src/Infrastructure/Dependencies.cs and src/Web/appsettings.json confirms the active provider is PostgreSQL (Npgsql), not SQL Server — see RC-001.

databases.engine: PostgreSQL via Npgsql.EntityFrameworkCore.PostgreSQL — CONFIRMED: src/Infrastructure/Dependencies.cs calls c.UseNpgsql(configuration.GetConnectionString("CatalogConnection")), and src/Web/appsettings.json CatalogConnection is 'Host=localhost;Port=5432;Database=eShopCatalog;Username=postgres;...'. Migration history also includes src/Infrastructure/Data/Migrations/20260526102924_InitialPostgres.cs. NOTE: src/Web/appsettings.Docker.json still defines a SQL Server-style connection string (Server=sqlserver,1433;...Initial Catalog=Microsoft.eShopOnWeb.CatalogDb) — this appears to be stale/legacy from the original SQL Server template and is not the provider actually registered in Dependencies.cs. See RC-001.

tables.notes: CatalogItemDetails struct exists alongside CatalogItem (src/ApplicationCore/Entities/CatalogItem.cs) — likely a DTO/value object, not its own table. INFERRED, confidence 0.7.

databases.context_class: AppIdentityDbContext (INFERRED — not present in the EF entity list supplied, but referenced by IdentityConnection string and standard ASP.NET Core Identity scaffolding used by eShopOnWeb)
databases.engine: PostgreSQL via Npgsql.EntityFrameworkCore.PostgreSQL — CONFIRMED: src/Infrastructure/Dependencies.cs calls options.UseNpgsql(configuration.GetConnectionString("IdentityConnection")), and src/Web/appsettings.json IdentityConnection is 'Host=localhost;Port=5432;Database=eShopIdentity;Username=postgres;...'. Migration src/Infrastructure/Identity/Migrations/20260526102952_InitialPostgres.Designer.cs confirms Postgres for AppIdentityDbContext too. appsettings.Docker.json's SQL Server string for IdentityConnection is likewise stale/legacy. See RC-001.

entities_not_mapped_to_tables.name: BaseEntity
entities_not_mapped_to_tables.reason: Abstract base class providing Id — not its own table. confidence 0.8

entities_not_mapped_to_tables.name: Buyer
entities_not_mapped_to_tables.reason: CONFIRMED NOT MAPPED — src/Infrastructure/Data/CatalogContext.cs declares exactly 7 DbSets (Baskets, CatalogItems, CatalogBrands, CatalogTypes, Orders, OrderItems, BasketItems), and src/Infrastructure/Identity/AppIdentityDbContext.cs's OnModelCreating only calls base.OnModelCreating — no Buyer/PaymentMethod configuration anywhere. Buyer.cs (src/ApplicationCore/Entities/BuyerAggregate/) is dead/aspirational code with no repository registration or DbSet. confidence 0.9. See RC-002.

entities_not_mapped_to_tables.name: PaymentMethod
entities_not_mapped_to_tables.reason: CONFIRMED NOT MAPPED — same evidence as Buyer above; PaymentMethod.cs exists only as a class referenced from Buyer.cs, with no DbSet, EntityTypeConfiguration, or repository usage found in src/. Dead/aspirational code. confidence 0.9. See RC-002.

entities_not_mapped_to_tables.name: CatalogItemDetails
entities_not_mapped_to_tables.reason: struct, likely a value object / DTO, not a table. confidence 0.75

entities_not_mapped_to_tables.name: Address
entities_not_mapped_to_tables.reason: Owned type embedded into Orders table (see Orders columns above). confidence 0.75

entities_not_mapped_to_tables.name: CatalogItemOrdered
entities_not_mapped_to_tables.reason: Owned type embedded into OrderItems table. confidence 0.75

change_records.what: Original said engine = 'SQL Server (azure-sql-edge in docker-compose)' for both CatalogDb and IdentityDb. CONFIRMED the active EF Core provider is PostgreSQL (Npgsql), per src/Infrastructure/Dependencies.cs (UseNpgsql) and src/Web/appsettings.json connection strings (Host=localhost;Port=5432;...). A separate SQL Server-style connection string still exists in appsettings.Docker.json, indicating the repo is mid-migration from SQL Server to Postgres and the Docker config is stale.

Original marked Buyer/PaymentMethod as UNKNOWN (confidence 0.7) whether mapped. CONFIRMED not mapped: CatalogContext.cs has exactly 7 DbSets and no Buyer/PaymentMethod; AppIdentityDbContext.OnModelCreating only calls base. Both classes are dead/aspirational code.
change_records.evidence_detail: src/Infrastructure/Data/CatalogContext.cs:14-20; src/Infrastructure/Identity/AppIdentityDbContext.cs:14-19; src/Infrastructure/Data/Migrations/CatalogContextModelSnapshot.cs (only Basket, BasketItem, CatalogBrand, CatalogItem, CatalogType, Order, OrderItem present)
